channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: WHO chief says he was at Yemen airport as Israeli bombs fell nearby
item.description: A wave of Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's main airport Thursday just as the World Health Organization’s director-general said he was about to board a flight there. One of the U.N. plane’s crew was wounded, WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's military intelligence says North Korean troops are suffering heavy battlefield losses
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — North Korean troops are suffering heavy losses in the fighting in Russia's Kursk region and facing logistical difficulties as a result of Ukrainian attacks, Ukraine’s military intelligence said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Moscow on Thursday warned against speculation that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan this week that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: India's former prime minister Manmohan Singh, architect of economic reforms, dies aged 92
item.description: India’s former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program and a landmark nuclear deal with the United States, has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia downplays speculation over deadly Azerbaijan Airlines crash as report lays blame for downed plane
item.description: Officials in Russia and Kazakhstan are urging people to wait for an investigation into the deadly Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash before assigning blame.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia arrests 4 suspects accused of plotting to kill top military officers on Ukraine's orders
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top security agency said Thursday that it has arrested several suspects accused of involvement in an alleged Ukrainian plot to assassinate senior military officers, an announcement that follows the killing of a top Russian general last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts say that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean opposition submits motion to impeach the country's acting president as strife deepens
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s main opposition party submitted a motion on Thursday to impeach the country’s acting leader over his reluctance to fill three Constitutional Court vacancies ahead of the court’s review of rebellion charges against impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol stemming fro
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Syrian family recounts the horrors of the 2013 chemical attack near Damascus
item.description: ZAMALKA, Syria (AP) — A Syrian family that survived a 2013 chemical weapons attack that killed hundreds of people near the country’s capital, Damascus, says the ordeal they experienced haunts them to this day. The Aug.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden condemns Russia's 'outrageous' Christmas attack on Ukraine
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday denounced Russia’s latest attack on Ukraine, declaring the wave of missiles and drones “outrageous.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prayers and tears mark 20 years since the Indian Ocean tsunami that killed some 230,000 people
item.description: People are gathering in prayer and visiting mass graves in Indonesia’s Aceh province to mark 20 years since the massive Indian Ocean tsunami, one of modern history’s worst natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Reza Saifullah And Edna Tarigan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope calls for nations to overcome divisions in Christmas address as communities celebrate worldwide
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis in his traditional Christmas message on Wednesday urged “all people of all nations” to find courage during this Holy Year “to silence the sounds of arms and overcome divisions” plaguing the world, from the Middle East to Ukraine, Africa to Asia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Desi Bouterse, a dictator convicted of murder who twice ruled Suriname, has died at 79
item.description: PARAMARIBO, Suriname (AP) — Desi Bouterse, a military strongman who led a 1980 coup in the former Dutch colony of Suriname then returned to power by election three decades later despite charges of drug smuggling and murder, has died. He was 79.
item.dc:creator: GEROLD ROZENBLAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Ukraine-Russia envoy slams Kremlin's Christmas Day attack
item.description: President-elect Trump’s choice for special envoy to Ukraine and Russia, retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg, slammed the Russian military missile and drone attack on Christmas Day and said the U.S. is “more resolved than ever to bring peace to the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope calls for end to global wars, divisions in Christmas message
item.description: Pope Francis called for an end to global wars and divisiveness in his annual Christmas Day message, given Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Shaheen breaks glass ceiling on Foreign Relations Committee
item.description: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen (D-N.H.) insists the Senate can chart a bipartisan course on global challenges under President-elect Trump, despite the Republican leader demonstrating his disruptive influence on the party even before reentering the White House.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US citizen imprisoned in Russia given new 15-year sentence in wake of espionage conviction
item.description: A Russian-born U.S. citizen who was already behind bars in Russia on a bribery conviction has been handed a new 15-year sentence for espionage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu wishes a merry Christmas to Christians around the world
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wished Christians worldwide a Merry Christmas and thanked the Christian community for its support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — In a dim one-room apartment in one of Istanbul’s poorest neighborhoods, 11-year-old Atakan Sahin curls up on a threadbare sofa with his siblings to watch TV while their mother stirs a pot of pasta. The simple meal is all the family of six can look forward to most evenings.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cities worldwide hold subdued Christmas Eve celebrations amid conflicts
item.description: BETHLEHEM, West Bank (AP) — Bethlehem marked another somber Christmas Eve on Tuesday in the traditional birthplace of Jesus under the shadow of war in Gaza. The cheer that typically descends on the West Bank during Christmas week were nowhere to be found.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian ship sinks in Mediterranean after explosion
item.description: A Russian cargo ship sank Monday in the Mediterranean Sea leaving two of the 16 crew members unaccounted for, according to the country’s authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
